behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: three collectors -> aggregation/prompt-builder. MOCK-MISS: a single-function unit test can miss dropped/overwritten source payloads between modules. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network required if source clients are in-process fakes, (b) assertion is on assembled input content, not model output, (c) no multi-agentic chain, (d) no planner/tool/critic loop. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System/Agentic because behavior text targets "content passed to the summary-generation step," not real transport or model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real collector-orchestration + prompt assembly modules with three fake source adapters returning distinct markers. Execute one report build and assert the exact payload sent to summary generation contains all three markers/sections.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: Failure is resilience across modules: source error -> orchestration -> section fallback rendering. MOCK-MISS: pure unit tests can miss exception propagation/translation across boundaries. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) source outage can be simulated by in-process adapter error; no real network required, (b) correctness does not depend on model quality, (c) no multi-agentic sequence, (d) no LLM-stage handoff logic under test. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior says "when one data source is unavailable" and is satisfiable via adapter failure simulation; no requirement for real infrastructure fault injection.
behaviors.test_description: Run report pipeline with two fake sources returning data and one fake source raising timeout/unavailable. Assert run completes, output includes a `data unavailable` note for only that section, and process does not abort.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP is direct: correctness is about semantic faithfulness of generated narrative to input values. A stubbed generator would always pass and hide real hallucination/omission failures (MOCK-HIDE). Not Workflow because this behavior describes one generation judgment step, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration due behavior text "accurately represents" narrative quality, which requires real model output.
behaviors.test_description: Feed fixed input datasets with known key values to the real summary model, run multiple trials, and score outputs against required facts (must include/align with key metrics, issue counts, deployment outcomes) with strict factual checks and tolerance rules.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA dominates: end-to-end delivery to a real inbox depends on real network/service behavior and email transport semantics. MOCK-HIDE: mocked email send can hide auth/routing/provider acceptance issues. Agentic is not minimum because acceptance criterion is inbox delivery correctness, not model-output quality. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic/Workflow since behavior text emphasizes "reaches ... inbox correctly" for full delivery path.
behaviors.test_description: Run scheduled job (or direct runner) in an environment hitting real/staging source endpoints and real/staging email provider, deliver to test mailbox, then assert message arrival, recipient list, subject, and body/report attachment integrity.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is state handoff/persistence across runs: send failure -> local persistence -> next-run retry logic. This can be reproduced with real modules plus fake mail adapter and temp filesystem. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no mandatory real network/subprocess if outage is simulated in adapter and runs are invoked in-process, (b) no dependence on model output, (c) no multi-agentic chain, (d) no LLM orchestration dependency. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text requires retry on next run, which is testable by two controlled invocations without real cron or provider outage.
behaviors.test_description: Use temp filesystem and real retry/persistence modules. First run: fake email client returns unreachable error; assert report file persisted. Second run: fake client succeeds; assert queued file is sent and cleared/marked delivered.
